name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC)
description: The DoD program that verifies defense contractors actually implement the cybersecurity requirements protecting FCI and CUI (FAR 52.204-21, NIST SP 800-171, NIST SP 800-172). CMMC 2.0 — codified at 32 CFR Part 170, effective December 16, 2024 — replaced the five-level 1.0 model with three levels tied directly to existing federal requirement sets.

highlights: Five maturity levels collapsed to three: L1 = 15 FAR 52.204-21 requirements (self-assessed), L2 = the 110 NIST SP 800-171 R2 requirements, L3 = +24 selected NIST SP 800-172 requirements (DIBCAC-assessed)
All CMMC-unique practices and the process-maturity dimension eliminated — CMMC is now purely a verification program for existing federal requirements
Self-assessment allowed at Level 1 and for select Level 2 programs; limited 180-day POA&Ms permitted
Annual senior-official affirmations in SPRS create direct False Claims Act exposure
Codified at 32 CFR Part 170 (effective Dec 16, 2024) with a phased contract rollout via the 48 CFR DFARS rule
Assessments verify against 800-171 Revision 2 — the Rev 3 transition timeline is a key watch item

changes.title: Maturity levels reduced from 5 to 3
changes.oldText: Five maturity levels: L1 Basic Cyber Hygiene, L2 Intermediate (transitional), L3 Good Cyber Hygiene, L4 Proactive (transitional), L5 Advanced/Progressive.
changes.newText: Three levels: Level 1 (Foundational), Level 2 (Advanced), Level 3 (Expert). The transitional levels 2 and 4 were eliminated.
changes.rationale: The 1.0 transitional levels were never intended to appear in contracts. Collapsing to three levels maps each level to an existing federal requirement set instead of CMMC-unique baselines.

changes.title: Maturity process requirements eliminated
changes.oldText: Each level required documented process maturity (e.g., ML.2.999 documented policies, ML.3.997 resourced plans) in addition to practices.
changes.rationale: CMMC 2.0 assesses practices only. The separate process-maturity dimension — a major source of 1.0 assessment complexity — was dropped entirely.

changes.title: CMMC-unique practices and domains removed (17 → 14 domains)
changes.oldText: CMMC 1.0 included 20 practices beyond NIST SP 800-171 at Level 3 (130 total) and 171 practices at Level 5, organized in 17 domains.
changes.newText: All requirements now trace directly to FAR 52.204-21, NIST SP 800-171 R2, or NIST SP 800-172 — no CMMC-unique practices. Domains align to 800-171 families (14).
changes.rationale: Contractors repeatedly objected that the delta-20 practices created a parallel standard. 2.0 makes CMMC purely a verification program for existing federal requirements.

changes.title: Level 1: self-assessment against the 15 FAR 52.204-21 safeguarding requirements
changes.oldText: Level 1 certification required a third-party assessment of 17 basic practices.
changes.newText: Level 1 requires an annual self-assessment of the 15 basic safeguarding requirements of FAR 52.204-21, plus an annual affirmation by a senior official in SPRS.
changes.rationale: Removes third-party assessment cost for the large population of contractors that handle only Federal Contract Information (FCI), not CUI.

changes.title: Level 2 = exactly the 110 requirements of NIST SP 800-171 R2
changes.oldText: Old Level 3 required 130 practices (110 from 800-171 plus 20 CMMC-unique).
changes.newText: Level 2 requires the 110 NIST SP 800-171 R2 requirements — nothing more. Assessment is either a C3PAO certification or, for select non-critical CUI programs, a self-assessment; both on a 3-year cycle with annual affirmations.
changes.rationale: Aligning exactly to 800-171 R2 lets contractors leverage existing DFARS 252.204-7012 work. Note: the program currently verifies against 800-171 Revision 2, not the newer Revision 3.

changes.title: Level 3 = 110 + 24 selected NIST SP 800-172 requirements, assessed by DIBCAC
changes.oldText: Old Levels 4–5 added 61 CMMC-unique advanced practices (171 total at L5).
changes.newText: Level 3 requires Level 2 certification plus 24 selected requirements from NIST SP 800-172, assessed by the DoD Industrial Base Cybersecurity Assessment Center (DIBCAC). POA&Ms are not permitted for the 24 enhanced requirements.
changes.rationale: Reserved for contractors supporting the most critical programs, with the government itself as assessor.

changes.title: POA&Ms conditionally allowed
changes.oldText: CMMC 1.0 required full compliance at the time of assessment — no Plans of Action and Milestones.
changes.newText: Limited POA&Ms are allowed: minimum assessment score required, only lower-weighted requirements eligible, and closure required within 180 days.
changes.rationale: A pragmatic concession — but the highest-weighted requirements (e.g., MFA-related) can never be on a POA&M.

changes.title: Waivers possible in limited circumstances
changes.newText: DoD may waive CMMC requirements on select mission-critical procurements, on a time-limited basis with senior DoD approval.
changes.rationale: Narrow by design; contractors should not plan around waivers.

changes.title: Annual affirmations in SPRS by a senior official
changes.newText: Every level requires an annual affirmation of continuing compliance by a senior company official, recorded in the Supplier Performance Risk System (SPRS).
changes.rationale: Creates direct False Claims Act exposure for false affirmations — arguably the sharpest enforcement tooth in the program.

changes.title: Program codified: 32 CFR Part 170 effective December 16, 2024
changes.oldText: CMMC 1.0 operated under interim DFARS rules (2020) without a codified program regulation.
changes.newText: The CMMC Program rule (32 CFR Part 170) was published October 15, 2024 and took effect December 16, 2024. The companion DFARS acquisition rule (48 CFR, clause 252.204-7021) triggers a phased rollout of CMMC requirements in contracts over roughly three years.
changes.rationale: Codification ended years of uncertainty. Requirements appear in solicitations in phases — starting with self-assessments and adding C3PAO certification and Level 3 requirements in later phases.
